cmd/jobs/billing/advance.purpose: Cobra sub-command package for invoice advance operations (list, advance single invoice, advance all). Accesses billing auto-advancement exclusively through the `internal.App` singleton which carries a `BillingAutoAdvancer` field wired by app/common.

patterns.name: internal.App singleton access
patterns.description: All service calls go through `internal.App.<Field>`, never construct services locally. The singleton is populated by Wire before Cobra executes.

patterns.name: cmd.Context() for context propagation
patterns.description: Always pass `cmd.Context()` to service calls — never `context.Background()` — so cancellation and deadlines from Cobra's context chain are respected.

patterns.name: Sub-command factory functions
patterns.description: Each sub-command is a `var <Name>Cmd = func() *cobra.Command { ... }` factory, not a top-level `var`. Allows deferred flag registration.
patterns.example: var ListCmd = func() *cobra.Command { cmd := &cobra.Command{...}; return cmd }

patterns.name: PersistentFlags on parent Cmd
patterns.description: Flags shared across sub-commands (e.g. `--namespace`) are registered on the parent `Cmd` via `Cmd.PersistentFlags()` in `init()`.

patterns.name: Nil slice for optional namespace filter
patterns.description: Namespace filter is a `[]string`; pass nil when empty so the service receives an unfiltered request, not an empty-slice filter.
patterns.example: var ns []string; if namespace != "" { ns = append(ns, namespace) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines parent Cmd plus ListCmd, InvoiceCmd, AllCmd factory functions for invoice advance operations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: InvoiceCmd requires --namespace flag to be non-empty; validate before calling AdvanceInvoice or you get a runtime error with no stack.

anti_patterns: Constructing billing services locally instead of using internal.App
Registering sub-command-specific flags on the parent Cmd (causes flag pollution across siblings)
Hardcoding namespace strings instead of accepting them via --namespace flag

decisions.decision: Thin Cobra wrappers delegating to internal.App service fields
decisions.rationale: Keeps cmd/* free of business logic; all wiring happens in app/common via Wire so each sub-command is independently testable and the DI graph is compile-time verified.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new advance sub-command that advances invoices for a specific customer

cmd/jobs/billing/advancecharges.purpose: Cobra sub-command package for charge advance operations (list customers with pending charges, advance a single customer, advance all). Guards every execution path against `internal.App.ChargesAutoAdvancer == nil` because charges are an optional feature.

patterns.name: Nil guard for optional features
patterns.description: Check `internal.App.ChargesAutoAdvancer == nil` at the start of every RunE; return a descriptive error instead of a nil pointer panic.
patterns.example: if internal.App.ChargesAutoAdvancer == nil { return fmt.Errorf("charges are not enabled") }

patterns.name: internal.App singleton access
patterns.description: All service calls go through `internal.App.<Field>`; never construct adapters locally.

patterns.name: cmd.Context() for context propagation
patterns.description: Pass `cmd.Context()` to every service call; never substitute `context.Background()`.

patterns.name: Sub-command factory functions
patterns.description: Each sub-command is a `var <Name>Cmd = func() *cobra.Command` factory registered in `init()` via `Cmd.AddCommand()`.

key_file_guides.role: Defines parent Cmd plus ListCmd, CustomerCmd, AllCmd for charge advancement. CustomerCmd requires --namespace and exactly one CUSTOMER_ID arg.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Every RunE must check `ChargesAutoAdvancer != nil` first; the field is nil when billing charges are disabled in config.

anti_patterns: Omitting the nil check on ChargesAutoAdvancer — causes panic when charges feature is disabled
Constructing charges services locally instead of using internal.App
Registering --namespace as a persistent flag on a sub-command instead of the parent Cmd

decisions.decision: Explicit nil check for optional ChargesAutoAdvancer field
decisions.rationale: Charges are a conditionally-wired feature; Wire produces a nil pointer when disabled. A nil guard yields a clear user-facing error instead of a runtime panic.

cmd/jobs/billing/collect.purpose: Cobra sub-command package for invoice collection operations (list collectable gathering invoices, collect for a specific customer, collect all). Uses structured input types from `billingworkercollect` rather than positional primitives.

patterns.name: Structured input types from domain packages
patterns.description: Pass `billingworkercollect.ListCollectableInvoicesInput` and `CollectCustomerInvoiceInput` structs rather than inline primitives, so the compiler enforces required fields.

patterns.name: time.Now() as default AsOf/CollectionAt
patterns.description: Collection operations default to `time.Now()` for temporal boundary; expose a flag if callers need to override.

patterns.name: StringSliceVar for multi-value filters
patterns.description: Multi-value filters (namespaces, customerIDs, invoiceIDs) use `cmd.PersistentFlags().StringSliceVar` accepting comma-separated or repeated flags.

patterns.name: Sub-command factory functions
patterns.description: Each sub-command is a factory var registered in `init()` via `Cmd.AddCommand()`.

key_file_guides.role: Defines parent Cmd plus ListCmd, InvoiceCmd, AllCmd for gathering invoice collection. InvoiceCmd hardcodes namespace='default' — a known limitation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: InvoiceCmd hardcodes `Namespace: "default"` for CustomerID; if multi-namespace support is needed, add a --namespace flag and pass it through.

anti_patterns: Hardcoding namespace strings (InvoiceCmd already has this debt — do not replicate it)
Inline construction of billing service instances instead of using internal.App
Passing nil CollectionAt — always supply time.Now() or a flag-driven value

decisions.decision: Use billingworkercollect input structs rather than individual parameters
decisions.rationale: Input structs evolve independently of the CLI; adding new fields to the struct does not require changing every call site.

cmd/jobs/billing/subscriptionsync.purpose: Cobra sub-command package for subscription-to-invoice sync reconciliation (list syncable subscriptions, sync all). Delegates entirely to `internal.App.BillingSubscriptionReconciler` using typed input from the `reconciler` package.

patterns.name: Typed reconciler input structs
patterns.description: Use `reconciler.ReconcilerListSubscriptionsInput` and embed it inside `reconciler.ReconcilerAllInput`; never pass primitives directly.

patterns.name: Shared filter flags across sub-commands via package-level vars
patterns.description: Filter vars (`namespaces`, `customerIDs`, `lookback`) are package-level and registered per sub-command via `cmd.PersistentFlags()`, not on the parent Cmd.

patterns.name: Default lookback constant
patterns.description: Define a `defaultLookback` constant (`24 * time.Hour`) and use it as the flag default so behavior is explicit and consistent.

patterns.name: Force flag for idempotent re-runs
patterns.description: AllCmd exposes a `--force` (`-f`) bool flag mapped to `ReconcilerAllInput.Force` to bypass sync-state checks for manual recovery.

key_file_guides.role: Defines parent Cmd plus ListCmd and AllCmd for subscription sync. AllCmd is the primary recovery tool for missed billing-worker events.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Package-level vars for filters are shared across sub-commands; re-registering them on both sub-commands means the last flag registration wins — avoid duplicate registration.

anti_patterns: Calling reconciler methods without the lookback filter — results in scanning all historical subscriptions
Constructing a reconciler instance locally instead of using internal.App.BillingSubscriptionReconciler
Omitting the --force flag on AllCmd — manual recovery runs need it

decisions.rationale: Keeps list and all-run filters structurally identical so the CLI flags are reusable and the reconciler API stays DRY.
